text:
The grandparents of British diver Tom Daley say they still get "butterflies" watching him dive but they 'couldn't be prouder' after watching him win his final Olympic medal.

Daley, 30, won his fifth Olympic medal after securing a silver alongside Noah Williams, 24, in the men's synchronised 10m platform in Paris on Monday morning.

The diver came out of retirement after his son, Robbie, six, said he wanted to see his dad dive at the Olympics.

Daley's grandparents Rosemary and David Daley, both 76,- watched on at home in Plymouth, Devon and couldn't be prouder.

David said: "We're highly delighted really.

"We knew it would be a problem to beat the Chinese.

"I think Tom dived much the same but it's just that the Chinese were that much better on the day.

"If the Chinese had slipped up on the last dive it would have changed everything."

Rosemary says she still gets nervous watching her grandson dive.

She said: "All the family are proud of him.

"I get butterflies watching him.

"I've always done that."

The grandparents were delighted to spot Daley's young family supporting him in Paris.

Rosemary said: "You could see him on the telly - little Robbie's eyes peeping over.

"I just hope he remembers it."

Rosemary and David said they know how proud Daley's late dad Robert would have been.

Robert died from a brain tumour, aged 40, just a few days after Daley's 17th birthday and the family remember fondly how much he loved to watch his son dive.

Rosemary said: "It's a happy sad story.

"When Robert wasn't so well and we knew it was coming to the end he wanted to watch him in China for one day.

"I remember one time we were at Stratford watching him. By then Robert was in a wheelchair.

"But when he won something everyone stood up - he said 'quick help me up'.

"He would insist on standing.

"What Robert did for Tom, I can see Tom doing it for his children.

"He's a really good dad.

"He wouldn't be pushy with them. Robert wasn't pushy.

"He would sit and watch and he'd be dead quiet.

"He was the one who took him everywhere and made sure he had water."

David said: "Tom's fame is a result of Robert's work."

The doting grandparents also praised Daley's diving partner.

Rosemary said: "He's lucky really he's got a partner with Noah because to find a partner to pair with Tom to replace Matty [Matty Lee] - hats off to Noah.

David added: "He's got to take as much of the credit because it takes two to do that."

title: Tom Daley's grandparents say they knew there would be one problem with Olympic gold medal | UK | News | Express.co.uk
author: Emma Dunn
description: Tom Daley's doting grandparents talk about how incredibly proud they are of their Olympian grandson.
